speaker: Argo
text: Oh.

speaker: Oscar
text: Ah.

speaker: Celio
text: Hello.

speaker: Professor Celio
text: When I stop by the library, Professor Celio and Oscar are there having a deep discussion about something.

speaker: Argo
text: Heh heh, I think I already know what you guys are talking about.

speaker: Oscar
text: “I-It’s not like we’re trying to hide it or anything. We’re talking about my novel.” *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: I have been giving Oscar some writing tips.

speaker: Celio
text: This is a new experience for me, but I’m learning quite a bit myself too. *sigh*

speaker: Oscar
text: “I’m sure I’m the one doing the most learning here.”

speaker: Argo
text: You’re with the author you respect, Oscar. I bet there's tons of stuff you can learn from him. 🐾

speaker: Celio
text: He has much better instincts than I do. He’ll be a better writer than me in no time. *sigh*

speaker: Oscar
text: Y-You flatter me… I’ve still got a ways to go…

speaker: Argo
text: (They both look like they’re having fun…) *sigh*

speaker: Argo
text: Can you afford to be studying novel writing instead of for the exam, Oscar? *sigh*

speaker: Oscar
text: I’m confident in my note-taking, so I think I’ll be fine.

speaker: Celio
text: I see. Well, I’m sure you will have excellent grades.

speaker: Oscar
text: You’re the one who should be studying instead of wandering around, right Argo? 🐾

speaker: Argo
text: Ugh... I'd rather not talk about it...

speaker: Oscar
text: By the way, who did you end up rooming with in the shuffle? ? *sigh*

speaker: Argo
text: That Paul guy, remember? From Harshreich?

speaker: Oscar
text: Paul...? Who was that again?

speaker: Argo
text: (Hmm, I guess he really is forgettable…)

speaker: Argo
text: What about you? Who did you get? *sigh*

speaker: Oscar
text: ...P-Prince Theo...

speaker: Argo
text: Really? Yikes...

speaker: Oscar
text: It’s just so uncomfortable. We haven't even had a single conversation… *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: That’s not good, Oscar. You must try to get to know each other. *sigh*

speaker: Oscar
text: I know, but... he's so hard to get along with… and I’m not exactly a social butterfly, either… *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: No excuses! Even if it leads to nothing, please make an effort! *

speaker: Oscar
text: Ugh... *sigh*

speaker: Argo
text: (Sharing a room with Theo...? You have my sympathy, Oscar...)

speaker: Argo
text: Did you have Professor Celio read your novel yet? *sigh*

speaker: Oscar
text: Yeah, he did.

speaker: Celio
text: It’s still rough around the edges, but it shows a lot of promise.

speaker: Argo
text: You hear that, Oscar?

speaker: Oscar
text: Eheheh. *

speaker: Celio
text: It would be nice to have more applicants like you, Oscar. I hope you write a magnificent knight story!

speaker: Oscar
text: I’ll do my best!

speaker: Celio
text: What do you hope to do in the future, Argo? *sigh*

speaker: Argo
text: Huh!? Hmm... It’s always been my dream to be a knight, but… *sigh*

speaker: Argo
text: Listening to you talk, I’m starting to feel like I should be thinking more about what I'll do after that… *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: That’s right. You know exactly what I’m talking about, don't you? *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: What you accomplish after being knighted… That’s what’s important. Becoming one is just the beginning. *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: Help those in need? Change something old? Or change something about yourself?

speaker: Celio
text: Your future is in your hands, Argo.

speaker: Celio
text: Do not worry! I’m sure you will make a fine knight. *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: But if you’re ever unsure about the future, please do not hesitate to talk to me. *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: We professors are here to provide guidance for the lives of our students. *sigh*

speaker: Argo
text: ...You're right.

speaker: Argo
text: I’ll make sure to ask for your help when I need it, Professor Celio! *sigh*

speaker: Oscar
text: ...*sigh*

speaker: Argo
text: By the way, you’re a noble, aren't you Professor Celio? *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: Hm...? What makes you think that? *sigh*

speaker: Argo
text: Like, your refined way of speaking… It doesn’t sound like how a commoner would talk. *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: I appreciate it, but I am not. *sigh*

speaker: Argo
text: Oh?

speaker: Oscar
text: But aren't knights also considered a semi-noble class? *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: Yes, that is correct.

speaker: Argo
text: What do you mean by "semi-noble"? *sigh*

speaker: Oscar
text: The title of knight gives you the same treatment nobles have.

speaker: Argo
text: Huh? So does that mean knights are all nobles?

speaker: Celio
text: Knighthood is a lifetime decoration that cannot be passed down, so we are not exactly nobles..

speaker: Oscar
text: It’s kind of like they’re between commoners and nobles.

speaker: Argo
text: Hmm, I think I understand... So in other words, it’s the class you become when you get knighted...?

speaker: Celio
text: Well, it’s just a title. One that’s needed to differentiate you from commoners once you are granted the privileges of knighthood. *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: Essentially, a title is something that adorns your identity. A convenient way to distinguish yourself.

speaker: Argo
text: Distinguish...? *sigh*

speaker: Oscar
text: You too will have the distinction once you’re a knight, Argo. *

speaker: Argo
text: Huh? Oh... Really!? B-But I don’t know if I really deserve it. *sigh*

speaker: Celio
text: Titles are all about what you do with them, Argo. You must find the best way to use yours. *sigh*

speaker: Argo
text: I see...

speaker: Argo
text: (But I grew up in a place that didn’t have things like that. I don’t really understand what it really means to have a title…) *sigh*

speaker: Argo
text: If you'll excuse me, Professor Celio. I should be going soon.

speaker: Celio
text: All right, thank you for your company.

speaker: Argo
text: Later, Oscar.

speaker: Oscar
text: Okay, see you at dinner.
